chapter_context.summary: The Philistine lords distrust David and refuse to let him join their army in the battle against King Saul and Israel.

outline.text: Philistines Assemble at Aphek; Princes Reject David
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Philistines Assemble at Aphek; Princes Reject David (29:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Achish Dismisses David; David Returns to Ziklag
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Achish Dismisses David; David Returns to Ziklag (29:6-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

key_themes.title: Sovereignty of God
key_themes.description: God raises up the humble (Hannah, Samuel, David) and humbles the proud (Eli's sons, Saul).

key_themes.title: The Heart vs. Outward Appearance
key_themes.description: God looks at the inner spiritual character, not physical stature or social status.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 29, how does this encourage you to trust God when circumstances look bleak?
How does this chapter prompt you to examine the sincerity of your own heart before the LORD?

cultural_historical.title: Transition to Monarchy
cultural_historical.text: Israel's request for a king represents a major cultural shift from a tribal league led by temporary judges to a centralized dynastic monarchy.
